title: Optical detector
abstract: An optical detector comprising a plurality of optical gain elements and a respective plurality of associated photodetectors, each optical gain element having a respective optical axis and each photodetector element having a respective optical axis, and wherein at least one of the plurality of optical gain elements is configured such that its optical axis is inclined with respect to the optical axis of an associated at least one of the photodetectors.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. An optical detector comprising a plurality of optical gain elements and a respective plurality of associated photodetectors, each optical gain element having a respective optical axis and each photodetector element having a respective optical axis, and wherein at least one of the plurality of optical gain elements is configured such that its optical axis is inclined with respect to the optical axis of an associated at least one of the photodetectors. 2. An optical detector according to claim 1 , wherein the optical axis of at least one optical gain element is inclined at an angle of inclination of greater than 5Â° with respect to the optical axis of the associated photodetector. 3. An optical detector according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the optical gain elements are configured such that each of their respective axes are inclined at a nonzero angle with respect to each other. 4. An optical detector as claimed in claim 3, wherein the angles between the respective optical axes of the optical gain elements and the respective optical axes of the respective photodetectors are incremented for each successive gain element, such as to provide a desired angle of coverage. 5. An optical detector according to any preceding claim, wherein the plurality of photodetectors is located in a planar array. 6. An optical detector according to claim 5, wherein the plurality of photodetectors is located in a printed circuit board. 7. An optical detector as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein there is provided an optical deflecting element between at least one optical gain element and an associated photodetector. 8. An optical detector as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the optical deflecting element is a prism. 9. An optical detector as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein at least one of the optical gain elements is a passive optical gain element. 10. An optical detector as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein at least one of the optical gain elements is an imaging concentrator. 1 1. An optical detector as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein at least one of the optical gain elements is a non-imaging concentrator. 12. An optical detector as claimed in claim 11 , wherein at least one of the optical gain elements is a Compound Parabolic Concentrator, CPC. 13. An optical detector as claimed in claim 1 1 , wherein at least one of the optical gain elements is a total internal reflection concentrator. 14. An optical detector according to any preceding claim wherein at least one of the optical gain elements is a lens. 15. An optical detector according to any preceding claim, wherein at least one of the optical gain elements is a Fresnel lens. 16. An optical detector according to any preceding claim, wherein at least one of the optical gain elements is a parabolic reflector. 17. An optical detector according to any preceding claim comprising a trans- impedance amplifier. 18. An optical detector according to claim 17, wherein each of the plurality of photodetectors has a respective trans-impedance amplifier. 19. An optical detector according to claim 16, wherein signals from at least two of the photodetectors are combined prior to input to the trans-impedance amplifier. 20. An optical detector according to any preceding claim, wherein at least one of the photodetectors is a PIN diode. 21. An optical detector according to any preceding claim, wherein at least one of the photodetectors is an avalanche photodiode.
